title: coreopsis aristulata
intro: Coreopsis aristulata, also known as the golden ragwort or tickseed, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and delicate foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Coreopsis aristulata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and act as a natural antihistamine, making it a potential remedy for allergies and other conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from a range of health concerns.
